content.full_text:
California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
Mr. Peter Savino 
P.O. Box 1871 
Twenty-nine Palms, CA 
92277 
Dear Mr. Savino: 
October 29, 1987 
Re: Your Request for Tnformal 
Assistance 
Our File No. 1-87-247 
You have requested information regarding your disclosure 
requirements under the conflict of interest disclosure 
provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").Y Also you 
inquired about the campaign disclosure requirements of the Act. 
In your letter of September 25, 1987, you indicated that 
you are a candidate for a position on the Board of Directors of 
Twenty-nine Palms Water District. 
You were given a Form 730, 
Statement of Economic Interests for Designated Employees, to be 
completed in conjunction with your candidacy. 
You asked for a 
copy of your disclosure category, a Form 730 Manual, and the 
Fair Political Practices Commission's "Guide to the Political 
Reform Act: California's Conflict of Interest Law for Public 
Officials." 
The 730 Manual and the "Guide" are enclosed. 
We are 
unable to supply you with a copy of your disclosure category 
because we do not have that information for Twenty-nine Palms 
Water District. 
Each agency is responsible for adopting a 
conflict of interest code. 
Therefore, you should contact the 
Water District or the Board of supervisors for your county to 
obtain a copy of your disclosure category. 
You asked about reviewing statements of economic 
interest. 
Each filing officer is required to conduct a facial 
review of all statements. 
Additionally, at least 20 percent of 
the original statements are subjected to a thorough review. 
(Regulation 18115, copy enclosed.) 
YGovernment Code sections 81000-91015. 
All statutory 
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise 
indicated. 
Commission regulations appear at 2 California 
Administrative Code section 18000, et seq. 
All references to 
regulations are to Title 2, Division of the California 
Administrative Code. 
428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804~0807 • (916) 322~5660 


Page Two 
Your letter of October 9, 1987, raised questions about 
campaign disclosure statements and information connected with an 
incorporation election for Twenty-nine Palms. 
Any campaign 
disclosure statements which must be filed by political 
committees involved in the incorporation election should be on 
file with your county's elections officials. 
(Section 84215.) 
Those statements are not filed with this office. 
If you have any questions regarding this letter, please 
feel free to call me at (916) 322-5662. 
Sincerely, 
Diane M. Griffiths 
General Counsel 
~W.~ 
By: Bruce W. Robeck 
Political Reform Consultant 


.. 
.. 


California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
Peter Savino 
P.o. Box 1871 
September 29, 1987 
Twenty-nine Palms, CA 
92277 
Re: 
87-247 
Dear Mr. Savino: 
Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform 
Act was received on September 28, 1987 by the Fair Political 
Practices Commission. 
If you have any questions about your 
advice request, you may contact me directly at (916) 322-5662. 
We try to answer all advice requests promptly. 
Therefore, 
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions, 
or more information is needed, you should expect a response 
within 21 working days if your request seeks formal written 
advice. 
If more information is needed, the person assigned to 
prepare a response to your request will contact you shortly to 
advise you as to the information needed. 
If your request is 
for informal assistance, we will answer it as quickly as we 
can. 
(See Commission Regulation 18329 (2 Cal. Adm. Code Sec. 
18329) .) 
You also should be aware that your letter and our response 
are public records which may be disclosed to the public upon 
receipt of a proper request for disclosure. 
JP: j aj 
Very truly yours,

,).~ r

/~J eanne Pr i tchard 
,~ Chief 
Technical Assistance and Analysis 
Division 
428 T Street. Suite 800 • 
P.O. Box 807 • 
Sacramento CA 95804~0807 • (916) 322~5660

sections.question: You have requested information regarding your disclosure requirements under the conflict of interest disclosure provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act"). Also you inquired about the campaign disclosure requirements of the Act. You were given a Form 730, Statement of Economic Interests for Designated Employees, to be completed in conjunction with your candidacy. You asked for a copy of your disclosure category, a Form 730 Manual, and the Fair Political Practices Commission's "Guide to the Political Reform Act: California's Conflict of Interest Law for Public Officials." Your letter of October 9, 1987, raised questions about campaign disclosure statements and information connected with an incorporation election for Twenty-nine Palms.
sections.conclusion: The 730 Manual and the "Guide" are enclosed. We are unable to supply you with a copy of your disclosure category because we do not have that information for Twenty-nine Palms Water District. Each agency is responsible for adopting a conflict of interest code. Therefore, you should contact the Water District or the Board of supervisors for your county to obtain a copy of your disclosure category. Each filing officer is required to conduct a facial review of all statements. Additionally, at least 20 percent of the original statements are subjected to a thorough review. Any campaign disclosure statements which must be filed by political committees involved in the incorporation election should be on file with your county's elections officials. Those statements are not filed with this office.
sections.question_synthetic: A candidate for the Twenty-nine Palms Water District Board of Directors requested information about conflict of interest disclosure requirements, Form 730 completion, his disclosure category, and campaign disclosure requirements related to an incorporation election.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC provided requested Form 730 materials and explained that disclosure categories must be obtained from the Water District or county supervisors. Campaign disclosure statements for the incorporation election must be filed with county election officials, not the FPPC.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document contains both an informal advice letter (October 29, 1987 from Bruce W. Robeck) and a preceding acknowledgment letter (September 29, 1987 from Jeanne Pritchard). Extracted primary substantive response. Question and conclusion synthesized from letter content as no formal Q/C sections labeled.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: You have requested information regarding your disclosure requirements under the conflict of interest disclosure provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act"). Also you inquired about the campaign disclosure requirements of the Act. You were given a Form 730, Statement of Economic Interests for Designated Employees, to be completed in conjunction with your candidacy. You asked for a copy of your disclosure category, a Form 730 Manual, and the Fair Political Practices Commission's "Guide to the Political Reform Act: California's Conflict of Interest Law for Public Officials." Your letter of October 9, 1987, raised questions about campaign disclosure statements and information connected with an incorporation election for Twenty-nine Palms.

CONCLUSION: The 730 Manual and the "Guide" are enclosed. We are unable to supply you with a copy of your disclosure category because we do not have that information for Twenty-nine Palms Water District. Each agency is responsible for adopting a conflict of interest code. Therefore, you should contact the Water District or the Board of supervisors for your county to obtain a copy of your disclosure category. Each filing officer is required to conduct a facial review of all statements. Additionally, at least 20 percent of the original statements are subjected to a thorough review. Any campaign disclosure statements which must be filed by political committees involved in the incorporation election should be on file with your county's elections officials. Those statements are not filed with this office.

embedding.summary: FPPC responds to Peter Savino, a candidate for Twenty-nine Palms Water District Board, providing guidance on conflict of interest disclosure requirements, Form 730 completion, and directing him to appropriate agencies for his disclosure category and campaign filing requirements.
